steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is "the constitution of Australia" — singular. After the introductory phrase, the blank must supply a singular pronoun plus a verb to connect the subject to the predicate "102nd in a global ranking."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — their: plural possessive pronoun; does not match the singular subject and supplies no verb. ✗
  • Choice B — they're: plural subject + verb; does not match the singular "constitution." ✗
  • Choice C — its: singular possessive, but possessive without a verb cannot serve as the subject + copula the sentence requires. ✗
  • Choice D — it's: contraction of "it is" — singular pronoun referring to "the constitution" plus the copular verb "is" needed to link the subject to "102nd." ✓
D is correct.
